commonIssues:
The mandatory Aadhaar-based e-KYC process often fails, especially for elderly citizens whose fingerprints are not recognized by e-POS machines.

How to fix:

  • Visit the nearest UIDAI Aadhaar Center to update your biometrics (iris and fingerprints).
  • Afterwards, re-attempt e-KYC at the Village Secretariat or try Iris authentication instead of fingerprints.

Many applications are rejected or cards suspended due to the strict "6-step validation" process, which uses data from other departments to check for electricity usage, land ownership, or vehicle ownership.

How to fix:

  • Request a "Re-verification" or submit an offline grievance at the Mandal Tahsildar office.
  • Provide proof of actual electricity consumption (bills) or property/vehicle documents to prove non-ownership of vehicles/land hindering eligibility.

Citizens report that their Aadhaar numbers are wrongly linked to someone else's high-consumption electricity connections, resulting in the wrongful cancellation of their cards.

How to fix:

  • Submit a discrepancy application at the nearest Village/Ward Secretariat with the correct electricity bill and your Aadhaar details.
  • Follow up to rectify the erroneous seeding and restore your eligibility status.

Newly married women face difficulties updating their names in the new place, often told to remove their name from the parent’s card, while others suffer from wrong age, gender, or name entries in the system.

How to fix:

  • First, formally delete the name from the old card using Aadhaar authentication or surrender certificate.
  • Then, apply for a 'Member Addition' through the Meeseva portal or Village Secretariat for the new family's card.

Frequent server downtime at the Village/Ward Secretariats and on the AePDS portal makes it difficult to apply, update, or download cards.

How to fix:

  • Portal load is typically lowest during early morning hours, so try accessing services then.
  • If urgent, utilize the offline application route through your local VRO (Village Revenue Officer).

As of April 2026, beneficiaries have faced delays in getting their ration rice due to technical issues in the supply chain.

How to fix:

  • Use the AePDS portal to reliably track stock availability before visiting.
  • If distribution is unacceptably delayed, contact your local grievance office or use the AP Civil Supplies toll-free helpline.

Families face issues in removing deceased members, which causes delays in updating family data for entitlement.

How to fix:

  • Formally obtain the death certificate from the municipality or panchayat immediately.
  • Submit a formal 'Member Deletion' request through your Village Secretariat along with the Aadhaar card of the head of the family.

A major issue arises when an applicant’s Aadhaar number is incorrectly linked to another person’s high-consumption electricity connection, leading to automated rejection for exceeding 300 units/month.

How to fix:

  • Visit the nearest Village/Ward Secretariat with a copy of your actual electricity bill and Aadhaar card.
  • Submit a discrepancy resolution form to delink the wrong meter connection. If unresolved, apply through the Navasakam beneficiary management portal.

The application website is frequently unresponsive or extremely slow, with servers often working only before 10 a.m. or after 5 p.m.

How to fix:

  • To avoid portal timeouts, try submitting your application online during off-peak hours (early morning 7 AM - 9 AM or late evening).
  • Alternatively, submit paper forms directly at your local Tahsildar (MRO) office.

Misinformation on family data, such as mapping new applicants to old addresses (e.g., a mother's or grandmother's village) rather than their current residential address.

How to fix:

  • You must first ensure your Aadhaar address is updated to your current address via the UIDAI portal.
  • Once Aadhaar reflects the new address, submit a "Migration/Address Change" application at your local Meeseva or Secretariat.

Many families discover that names of eligible members are missing from the draft beneficiary lists.

How to fix:

  • Initiate an "Addition of Family Member" request on the AP Civil Supplies portal or via Meeseva.
  • Provide the missing member's Aadhaar card and the birth certificate for minors. e-KYC authentication will be mandatory for the added member.

Women married across state borders often face difficulty removing their names from their parental home's ration card, with officials sometimes insisting on a death certificate to delete entries.

How to fix:

  • Obtain a marriage certificate and a formal Surrender/Deletion Certificate from the parental state's Civil Supplies office.
  • Submit these documents physically to your local MRO/Tahsildar to process the deletion and subsequent addition correctly.

Shortages of staff at Grama/Ward Secretariats and lack of awareness about new regulations among employees lead to long delays in processing.

How to fix:

  • If your application is stuck beyond the 30-day SLA (Service Level Agreement), escalate the issue via the "Spandana" grievances portal (spandana.ap.gov.in).
  • Alternatively, call the toll-free collectorate helpline (1902) to compel action.

Genuine applicants are marked ineligible due to errors in the government’s 6-step validation, which wrongly identifies them as owning four-wheelers, paying income tax, or being government employees.

How to fix:

  • Submit a "Re-verification" request through the Navasakam portal or Secretariat.
  • Provide an Income Certificate to prove lack of income tax liability, or a statement from the RTO proving the sold/scrapped status of the flagged four-wheeler vehicle.

Applicants are forced to "run from pillar to post" between Civil Supplies offices, MRO offices, and secretariats because data corrections are not updated promptly.

How to fix:

  • Whenever submitting documents, demand an acknowledgment receipt or tracking number.
  • Use this application number to track status online via the epds.ap.gov.in portal instead of visiting physically. If no action is taken, escalate via the Spandana grievance mechanism.

Initial promised dates for card distribution are often postponed, leaving families waiting for months to receive their rice cards.

How to fix:

  • If your card is officially "Approved" online but you haven't received the physical card, you can still draw rations using your Aadhaar number and biometric authentication at the Fair Price Shop (FPS).
  • The digital approval is fully valid.

Due to errors in the card, many applicants are unable to avail of government welfare schemes and subsidized rice.

How to fix:

  • Ensure that your e-KYC is active and bank account is NPCI-mapped (Aadhaar Seeded) for Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT).
  • Most welfare blockages are caused by failed e-KYC or inactive bank mapping rather than the ration card issuance itself.

Applicants allege that local officials often respond with indifference, failing to resolve the inaccuracies in the system.

How to fix:

  • Bypass local officials by launching complaints directly on the Chief Minister's official grievance cell (Jaganannaku Chebudam) at 1902.
  • Alternatively, escalate through the AP Civil Supplies toll-free helpline at 1967 or 1800-425-2977.
